text.title: Data-Driven Requirements Engineering Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real engagements applying data-driven-requirements. Each entry pairs symptom + root cause + fix.

antipattern.symptom: Prioritization driven by HiPPO (highest paid person's opinion).
antipattern.fix: Lock RICE/WSJF inputs to baseline + target metrics; HiPPO veto requires written justification.

antipattern.symptom: Target uses a vanity metric (page views, sign-ups) with no causal link to business outcome.
antipattern.root-cause: Author picks the easiest metric to move.
antipattern.fix: Replace with North-Star aligned metric; reject targets that don't move retention/revenue/cost.

antipattern.symptom: Requirement has a target but no baseline.
antipattern.root-cause: Schedule pressure to push spec before measurement.
antipattern.fix: Block requirement until baseline_exists == true; otherwise the post-launch measurement is meaningless.

antipattern.symptom: Spec ships without dashboards or events.
antipattern.root-cause: BA defers instrumentation to engineering and it never happens.
antipattern.fix: Make instrumentation a mandatory section; review with analytics-engineering before sign-off.
